descriptor: No documented revenue model; pricing is ad hoc or undefined
evidence_criteria: No written revenue model exists; pricing decisions made case-by-case

descriptor: Basic revenue model articulated but not validated or consistently applied
evidence_criteria: Written revenue model document exists; at least one pricing tier defined

descriptor: Revenue model documented with clear pricing tiers and monetization strategy
evidence_criteria: Revenue model document with pricing tiers, value metrics, and monetization logic reviewed within last 12 months

descriptor: Revenue model actively tested and optimized with data-driven pricing experiments
evidence_criteria: Evidence of pricing experiments or A/B tests; revenue model review cadence with results tracked; unit economics per tier documented

descriptor: Revenue model continuously refined with predictive analytics and market-leading pricing innovation
evidence_criteria: Predictive revenue modeling in place; dynamic pricing or value-based pricing fully implemented; quarterly model review with board-level reporting

descriptor: Unit economics not tracked; no visibility into CAC, LTV, or margins
evidence_criteria: No unit economics dashboard or calculations exist

descriptor: Basic unit economics calculated periodically but not systematically tracked
evidence_criteria: CAC and LTV calculated at least once; basic spreadsheet or document exists

descriptor: Unit economics tracked monthly with defined CAC, LTV, and contribution margin metrics
evidence_criteria: Monthly unit economics report or dashboard; CAC, LTV, gross margin, and contribution margin all tracked; definitions documented

descriptor: Unit economics integrated into decision-making with cohort analysis and segment-level tracking
evidence_criteria: Cohort-based LTV analysis; segment-level CAC tracking; unit economics influence product and marketing decisions with documented examples

descriptor: Predictive unit economics with real-time dashboards and automated alerting on margin shifts
evidence_criteria: Real-time unit economics dashboard; predictive models for LTV and CAC; automated alerts on metric degradation; quarterly optimization reviews

descriptor: Cash flow not forecasted; reactive management of cash position
evidence_criteria: No cash flow forecast exists; bank balance checked ad hoc

descriptor: Basic cash flow tracking with simple short-term forecasting
evidence_criteria: Monthly cash flow statement produced; basic 30-day forecast exists

descriptor: Regular cash flow forecasting with 90-day rolling projections and variance tracking
evidence_criteria: 90-day rolling cash flow forecast updated at least bi-weekly; variance analysis performed monthly; AR/AP aging tracked

descriptor: Proactive cash flow optimization with scenario modeling and working capital management
evidence_criteria: Scenario-based cash flow models (best/worst/expected); working capital optimization initiatives documented; cash conversion cycle tracked

descriptor: Automated cash flow management with real-time visibility and predictive treasury operations
evidence_criteria: Real-time cash position dashboard; automated cash flow forecasting with ML-driven predictions; treasury policy documented and reviewed quarterly

descriptor: No formal budgeting or financial planning process
evidence_criteria: No annual budget or financial plan exists

descriptor: Annual budget created but rarely updated; limited variance analysis
evidence_criteria: Annual budget document exists; some comparison of actual vs. budget performed

descriptor: Quarterly financial planning with monthly variance analysis and basic scenario planning
evidence_criteria: Quarterly reforecast process documented; monthly variance reports with explanations; at least two scenarios modeled annually

descriptor: Integrated FP&A with driver-based models, rolling forecasts, and cross-functional input
evidence_criteria: Driver-based financial model; rolling 12-month forecast; cross-functional planning inputs documented; FP&A calendar with stakeholder sign-off

descriptor: Continuous planning with real-time data integration, automated reporting, and strategic scenario analysis
evidence_criteria: Real-time financial dashboards; automated variance detection; strategic scenario planning tied to board reporting; FP&A team with dedicated tooling

descriptor: Pricing set intuitively with no market or cost analysis
evidence_criteria: No pricing analysis or documentation exists; prices set by founder intuition

descriptor: Basic pricing established with some competitor or cost-based analysis
evidence_criteria: Competitor pricing comparison exists; cost-plus or simple markup logic documented

descriptor: Pricing strategy documented with market positioning rationale and regular review cadence
evidence_criteria: Pricing strategy document with positioning rationale; annual pricing review process; customer willingness-to-pay data collected

descriptor: Data-driven pricing optimization with segmented pricing and elasticity analysis
evidence_criteria: Price elasticity analysis performed; segment-specific pricing tiers; pricing experiments documented with results; pricing committee or owner defined

descriptor: Dynamic, value-based pricing with continuous optimization and competitive moat
evidence_criteria: Value-based pricing methodology implemented; dynamic pricing or usage-based models with real-time adjustment; quarterly pricing optimization reviews with revenue impact analysis

descriptor: No visibility into cost structure; spending decisions made without analysis
evidence_criteria: No cost breakdown or analysis exists

descriptor: Basic cost categories identified; some awareness of major cost drivers
evidence_criteria: Cost categories documented; major cost drivers identified in at least one review

descriptor: Cost structure documented with regular reviews and identified optimization opportunities
evidence_criteria: Cost structure breakdown by category; quarterly cost reviews; at least two optimization initiatives identified and tracked

descriptor: Proactive cost management with benchmarking, vendor negotiations, and efficiency targets
evidence_criteria: Industry benchmarking data used; vendor renegotiation cadence documented; efficiency targets set and tracked; cost per unit metrics monitored

descriptor: Continuous cost optimization with automated monitoring and zero-based budgeting practices
evidence_criteria: Automated cost monitoring with anomaly alerts; zero-based or activity-based costing implemented; cost optimization tied to strategic planning; quarterly efficiency gains reported

descriptor: Single revenue stream with high concentration risk
evidence_criteria: Revenue from one product/service or one major customer exceeds 80%

descriptor: Awareness of concentration risk; initial exploration of additional revenue streams
evidence_criteria: Concentration risk acknowledged in planning documents; at least one new revenue stream being explored

descriptor: Two or more revenue streams contributing meaningfully; diversification strategy documented
evidence_criteria: At least two revenue streams each contributing >10% of revenue; diversification strategy documented with targets

descriptor: Multiple revenue streams actively managed with segment-level P&L and portfolio approach
evidence_criteria: Three or more revenue streams; segment-level P&L tracking; portfolio-level revenue management with investment allocation rationale

descriptor: Balanced revenue portfolio with systematic new-stream incubation and no single-source dependency
evidence_criteria: No single revenue stream exceeds 40%; systematic new revenue stream incubation process; quarterly portfolio rebalancing reviews; geographic or market diversification

descriptor: Financial reporting is informal, late, or inaccurate; no internal controls
evidence_criteria: No regular financial reports produced; no documented controls or processes

descriptor: Basic financial reports produced but often late or incomplete; minimal controls
evidence_criteria: Monthly financial statements produced (even if delayed); basic bank reconciliation performed

descriptor: Timely monthly financial reporting with documented controls and reconciliation processes
evidence_criteria: Monthly close within 15 business days; documented accounting policies; bank and key account reconciliations performed monthly; chart of accounts defined

descriptor: Comprehensive reporting with segregation of duties, audit-ready processes, and management dashboards
evidence_criteria: Monthly close within 10 business days; segregation of duties documented; annual audit completed; management dashboard with KPIs; internal control matrix documented

descriptor: Real-time financial reporting with automated controls, continuous auditing, and board-ready packages
evidence_criteria: Monthly close within 5 business days; automated control testing; continuous auditing processes; board-ready financial packages; SOX-equivalent controls or SOC 2 compliance

descriptor: Capital allocation decisions made ad hoc without framework or criteria
evidence_criteria: No capital allocation framework; investment decisions made by intuition or urgency

descriptor: Basic awareness of capital allocation; some prioritization of spending
evidence_criteria: Annual budget allocates capital to categories; some discussion of ROI before major investments

descriptor: Capital allocation framework with defined criteria and regular review of investment performance
evidence_criteria: Written capital allocation criteria (ROI thresholds, strategic fit); quarterly investment reviews; portfolio of investments tracked with expected vs. actual returns

descriptor: Disciplined capital allocation with portfolio optimization, hurdle rates, and stage-gate governance
evidence_criteria: Defined hurdle rates by investment type; stage-gate process for large investments; portfolio view of all capital commitments; post-investment reviews documented

descriptor: Strategic capital allocation with real-option thinking, dynamic rebalancing, and value-creation tracking
evidence_criteria: Real-option or DCF analysis for major investments; dynamic portfolio rebalancing process; value-creation tracking against strategic plan; board-level capital allocation review quarterly

descriptor: No structured investor or stakeholder communication; updates are reactive
evidence_criteria: No regular investor updates; communication happens only when investors ask

descriptor: Periodic investor updates sent but inconsistent in format and cadence
evidence_criteria: At least quarterly investor updates sent; basic financial summary included

descriptor: Regular, structured investor communication with consistent reporting and proactive updates
evidence_criteria: Monthly or quarterly investor report template; proactive communication of material events; KPI dashboard shared with investors; stakeholder list maintained

descriptor: Strategic stakeholder management with tailored communication, board materials, and relationship tracking
evidence_criteria: Board materials prepared with strategic context; stakeholder-specific communication plans; investor CRM or tracking system; annual investor day or strategic review

descriptor: Best-in-class investor relations with proactive narrative management and institutional-grade reporting
evidence_criteria: Institutional-grade reporting package; proactive IR strategy with media coordination; benchmarking against public-company IR standards; NPS or satisfaction survey from stakeholders

descriptor: No documented product vision; direction changes frequently
evidence_criteria: No written product vision or strategy document exists

descriptor: Basic product vision exists but not widely shared or used to guide decisions
evidence_criteria: Product vision statement written; some team members aware of it

descriptor: Clear product vision documented and shared with the team; strategy tied to business goals
evidence_criteria: Product vision and strategy document reviewed within 12 months; team can articulate the vision; strategy linked to business objectives

descriptor: Product vision drives roadmap decisions with measurable strategic outcomes and regular validation
evidence_criteria: Vision-to-roadmap traceability documented; strategic outcomes measured quarterly; vision validated with customer and market data; cross-functional alignment sessions held

descriptor: Inspiring product vision that drives company strategy with continuous market validation and thought leadership
evidence_criteria: Product vision influences company-level strategy; continuous market validation loop; thought leadership content published; annual vision refresh with board input

descriptor: No systematic customer research; assumptions drive product decisions
evidence_criteria: No customer interviews, surveys, or research conducted in the last 6 months

descriptor: Some customer conversations happen but informally and without consistent methodology
evidence_criteria: At least some customer interviews conducted; informal notes or summaries exist

descriptor: Regular customer discovery with documented methodology and synthesis of insights
evidence_criteria: Customer interview process documented; at least 10 interviews per quarter; insight synthesis shared with product team; personas or JTBD documented

descriptor: Continuous customer discovery integrated into product development with cross-functional participation
evidence_criteria: Engineers and designers participate in customer research; research repository maintained; insights directly influence roadmap with documented examples; discovery sprints scheduled

descriptor: Customer-obsessed discovery process with predictive insights, ethnographic research, and market-shaping innovation
evidence_criteria: Ethnographic or contextual inquiry methods used; predictive customer need analysis; customer advisory board active; research influences strategic direction beyond product

descriptor: No product roadmap; work is decided week to week
evidence_criteria: No roadmap document or tool exists; priorities shift constantly

descriptor: Basic roadmap exists as a feature list but lacks strategic framing or timeline
evidence_criteria: A list of planned features or projects exists; some rough sequencing defined

descriptor: Outcome-driven roadmap with clear themes, time horizons, and stakeholder communication
evidence_criteria: Roadmap organized by themes or outcomes; now/next/later or quarterly time horizons; roadmap shared with stakeholders regularly; linked to product strategy

descriptor: Dynamic roadmap with data-driven prioritization, capacity planning, and cross-functional input
evidence_criteria: Roadmap updated based on data and customer feedback; capacity planning integrated; cross-functional input process documented; roadmap review cadence established with leadership

descriptor: Continuously optimized roadmap with OKR alignment, predictive planning, and portfolio-level management
evidence_criteria: Roadmap tied to OKRs with measurable outcomes; predictive capacity and impact modeling; multi-product portfolio roadmap management; quarterly roadmap retrospectives

descriptor: Features prioritized by loudest voice or founder preference
evidence_criteria: No prioritization framework; decisions made ad hoc by HiPPO

descriptor: Some prioritization criteria discussed but not consistently applied
evidence_criteria: Team has discussed prioritization approaches; some use of impact/effort or similar heuristics

descriptor: Defined prioritization framework applied consistently with documented decision rationale
evidence_criteria: Prioritization framework documented (RICE, ICE, weighted scoring, or equivalent); applied to last 3+ prioritization decisions with rationale recorded

descriptor: Data-driven prioritization with quantified impact, opportunity cost analysis, and stakeholder alignment
evidence_criteria: Quantified impact estimates for prioritized features; opportunity cost considered; stakeholder input process defined; prioritization decisions reviewed for accuracy retroactively

descriptor: Predictive prioritization with continuous recalibration, portfolio optimization, and impact tracking
evidence_criteria: Predictive impact models validated against actuals; continuous reprioritization triggers defined; portfolio-level feature investment tracking; prioritization framework evolves based on retrospective data

descriptor: No measurement of product-market fit; assumption that the product is needed
evidence_criteria: No PMF metrics tracked; no customer retention or satisfaction data collected

descriptor: Anecdotal evidence of product-market fit from customer conversations
evidence_criteria: Some positive customer testimonials; basic retention data available

descriptor: PMF measured with defined metrics including retention, NPS, and Sean Ellis test
evidence_criteria: PMF survey (Sean Ellis or equivalent) conducted; retention cohorts tracked; NPS measured; results documented and shared

descriptor: Multi-dimensional PMF measurement with segment-level analysis and leading indicators
evidence_criteria: Segment-level PMF analysis; leading indicators of PMF identified and tracked; PMF dashboard; correlation between PMF metrics and revenue growth documented

descriptor: Continuous PMF optimization with predictive churn models and market expansion validation
evidence_criteria: Predictive churn and engagement models; PMF validated for each new segment or market; PMF score influences investment decisions; quarterly PMF health report to leadership

descriptor: No intentional UX design; interface built by engineers without user research
evidence_criteria: No designer on team; no usability testing or user research for design decisions

descriptor: Some design attention but inconsistent; basic wireframes or mockups created
evidence_criteria: Mockups or wireframes created for some features; at least one designer or design-minded team member involved

descriptor: Consistent UX design process with user testing, design system foundations, and accessibility basics
evidence_criteria: Design process documented; usability testing conducted at least quarterly; basic design system or component library exists; WCAG AA basics addressed

descriptor: Mature UX practice with comprehensive design system, regular user research, and cross-platform consistency
evidence_criteria: Full design system maintained; regular usability studies with documented findings; cross-platform design consistency; UX metrics (task completion, time on task) tracked

descriptor: Industry-leading UX with continuous optimization, accessibility excellence, and design-driven differentiation
evidence_criteria: WCAG AAA compliance; continuous UX optimization program; design thinking workshops with customers; UX benchmarked against industry leaders; design a recognized competitive advantage

descriptor: No product analytics; decisions made without usage data
evidence_criteria: No analytics tool implemented; no usage metrics tracked

descriptor: Basic analytics tool in place but sparse instrumentation and limited usage
evidence_criteria: Analytics tool installed (e.g., Google Analytics, Mixpanel); some page views or basic events tracked

descriptor: Key user journeys instrumented with defined metrics and regular reporting
evidence_criteria: Core user journeys instrumented with events; key metrics defined (DAU, retention, conversion); weekly or monthly analytics review; dashboard accessible to product team

descriptor: Comprehensive instrumentation with funnel analysis, cohort tracking, and experiment infrastructure
evidence_criteria: Full event taxonomy documented; funnel analysis for key flows; cohort-based retention tracking; A/B testing infrastructure in place with documented experiments

descriptor: Predictive analytics with real-time dashboards, automated insights, and data-driven product culture
evidence_criteria: Real-time product analytics dashboard; automated anomaly detection; predictive models for user behavior; data literacy across product org; analytics influence >80% of product decisions

descriptor: No competitive awareness; competitors not monitored
evidence_criteria: No competitor list or analysis exists

descriptor: Ad hoc competitive awareness from sales conversations and news
evidence_criteria: Key competitors identified; informal knowledge from sales team or news

descriptor: Regular competitive analysis with documented feature comparisons and market positioning
evidence_criteria: Competitive landscape document maintained; feature comparison matrix; quarterly competitive review; win/loss analysis includes competitive data

descriptor: Proactive competitive intelligence with market monitoring, battlecards, and strategic response planning
evidence_criteria: Competitive battlecards for sales team; automated market monitoring; strategic response playbook for competitive moves; competitive insights influence roadmap

descriptor: Market-shaping competitive strategy with predictive analysis and category leadership
evidence_criteria: Predictive competitive modeling; category creation or leadership strategy; competitive intelligence team or dedicated function; competitive position reviewed at board level

descriptor: No structured feedback collection; occasional anecdotal input from customers
evidence_criteria: No feedback collection process; customer input is random and unstructured

descriptor: Some feedback collected through support tickets or ad hoc surveys
evidence_criteria: Feedback gathered from support channels; occasional survey or feedback form exists

descriptor: Structured feedback loop with collection, categorization, and regular synthesis into product decisions
evidence_criteria: Feedback collection across multiple channels; categorization and tagging system; monthly feedback synthesis shared with product team; feedback influences roadmap with examples

descriptor: Closed-loop feedback system with customer follow-up, prioritization integration, and trend analysis
evidence_criteria: Closed-loop process where customers are notified of action taken; feedback integrated into prioritization framework; trend analysis performed quarterly; VoC program documented

descriptor: Predictive feedback system with sentiment analysis, proactive outreach, and customer co-creation
evidence_criteria: Automated sentiment analysis; proactive feedback solicitation triggered by usage patterns; customer co-creation programs; feedback system ROI measured; NPS response rate >40%

descriptor: No defined quality standards; bugs ship frequently
evidence_criteria: No acceptance criteria or quality gates; customer-reported bugs are primary quality signal

descriptor: Basic quality awareness with some testing but inconsistent standards
evidence_criteria: Some manual testing before release; quality issues discussed but no formal standards

descriptor: Defined quality standards with acceptance criteria, release gates, and bug severity classification
evidence_criteria: Acceptance criteria required for features; release checklist or gate process; bug severity classification system; quality metrics tracked (defect rate, escape rate)

descriptor: Comprehensive quality program with automated testing, SLAs for bug resolution, and quality dashboards
evidence_criteria: Automated test coverage >60%; bug resolution SLAs defined and tracked; quality dashboard visible to team; regression testing process; quality retrospectives conducted

descriptor: Quality excellence with shift-left testing, predictive defect analysis, and zero-critical-bug release policy
evidence_criteria: Shift-left testing culture with >80% automated coverage; predictive defect analysis; zero-critical-bug release policy enforced; quality metrics benchmarked against industry; customer-perceived quality measured

descriptor: No structured approach to exploring new product ideas beyond the current roadmap
evidence_criteria: No innovation process or pipeline exists; all work is on current product features

descriptor: Occasional brainstorming sessions; some ad hoc experimentation
evidence_criteria: Some hackathons or brainstorm sessions held; at least one experimental idea explored informally

descriptor: Defined innovation process with idea intake, evaluation criteria, and dedicated time allocation
evidence_criteria: Innovation pipeline process documented; idea submission mechanism exists; evaluation criteria defined; allocated time (e.g., 10% time, innovation sprints) for exploration

descriptor: Active innovation portfolio with stage-gated experiments, success metrics, and cross-functional teams
evidence_criteria: Stage-gate process for innovation ideas; active portfolio of experiments tracked; success metrics defined per experiment; cross-functional innovation teams formed; budget allocated

descriptor: Systematic innovation engine with external partnerships, patent strategy, and new-market incubation
evidence_criteria: Innovation lab or dedicated team; external partnerships for R&D; patent or IP strategy; new-market incubation with validated playbook; innovation ROI measured and reported to board

descriptor: No defined ideal customer profile; selling to anyone who will buy
evidence_criteria: No ICP document exists; customer acquisition is opportunistic

descriptor: Informal understanding of best-fit customers but not documented or consistently applied
evidence_criteria: Sales team can describe good-fit customers verbally; some anecdotal patterns identified

descriptor: ICP documented with firmographics, behavior patterns, and buying signals; used in lead qualification
evidence_criteria: Written ICP with firmographics, pain points, and buying signals; ICP used in lead scoring or qualification criteria; reviewed within last 12 months

descriptor: Data-validated ICP with segment-level profiles, win/loss analysis, and predictive lead scoring
evidence_criteria: ICP validated with win/loss data; multiple ICP segments defined; predictive lead scoring model incorporates ICP attributes; ICP refresh cadence established

descriptor: Dynamic ICP with real-time signals, propensity modeling, and market expansion targeting
evidence_criteria: Real-time ICP signal monitoring; propensity-to-buy models; ICP drives market expansion decisions; automated ICP-based lead routing; quarterly ICP optimization with revenue correlation

descriptor: No defined sales process; each rep sells differently
evidence_criteria: No documented sales stages or methodology; inconsistent approaches across the team

descriptor: Basic sales stages defined but inconsistently followed
evidence_criteria: Sales stages named and defined in CRM or document; some reps follow the process

descriptor: Documented sales process with stage criteria, exit requirements, and methodology training
evidence_criteria: Sales process documented with entry/exit criteria per stage; methodology (MEDDIC, Challenger, SPIN, etc.) selected and trained; >70% of deals follow the process

descriptor: Optimized sales process with data-driven stage management, playbooks, and continuous coaching
evidence_criteria: Stage conversion rates tracked and optimized; deal playbooks for key segments; regular coaching sessions documented; process compliance >90%; methodology reinforced through tooling

descriptor: Predictive sales process with AI-assisted next-best-actions and continuous methodology evolution
evidence_criteria: AI-assisted deal insights and next-best-action recommendations; methodology continuously refined based on win/loss data; sales process benchmarked against industry best practice; new hire ramp time measured and optimized

descriptor: No pipeline visibility; revenue forecasts are guesses
evidence_criteria: No pipeline tracking in CRM; no formal forecast process

descriptor: Basic pipeline tracked in CRM but data quality is poor and forecasts unreliable
evidence_criteria: CRM has pipeline data; some forecast produced but accuracy is low; pipeline reviews happen sporadically

descriptor: Regular pipeline reviews with defined stages, weighted forecasting, and data hygiene standards
evidence_criteria: Weekly pipeline reviews conducted; weighted forecast model applied; data hygiene standards documented; forecast accuracy tracked; pipeline coverage ratio monitored

descriptor: Advanced pipeline management with multi-scenario forecasting, velocity metrics, and deal scoring
evidence_criteria: Multi-scenario forecasts (commit/best-case/upside); pipeline velocity metrics tracked; deal scoring model in place; forecast accuracy within 15%; pipeline analytics dashboard

descriptor: Predictive pipeline management with AI-driven forecasting and automated pipeline health monitoring
evidence_criteria: AI-driven forecast models; automated pipeline health alerts; forecast accuracy within 10%; pipeline-to-capacity planning integration; real-time pipeline dashboards for leadership

descriptor: No structured lead generation; leads come from founder network or inbound inquiries
evidence_criteria: No lead gen programs or campaigns; lead source is word-of-mouth or ad hoc

descriptor: Some lead generation activity but inconsistent volume and no qualification criteria
evidence_criteria: At least one lead gen channel active (content, ads, outbound); some leads generated monthly; no formal qualification criteria

descriptor: Multi-channel lead generation with defined MQL/SQL criteria and lead scoring
evidence_criteria: Multiple lead gen channels active; MQL and SQL definitions documented; lead scoring model in place; monthly lead volume tracked by source; marketing-to-sales handoff process defined

descriptor: Optimized lead generation with channel ROI analysis, automated nurturing, and predictive qualification
evidence_criteria: Channel-level CAC and ROI tracked; automated lead nurturing workflows; predictive lead qualification; lead-to-opportunity conversion rates optimized by segment; SLA between marketing and sales

descriptor: Demand engine with intent-based targeting, account-based marketing, and full-funnel optimization
evidence_criteria: Intent data integrated into targeting; ABM program for strategic accounts; full-funnel attribution model; demand generation influences >60% of pipeline; automated lead routing with sub-5-minute response time

descriptor: No sales enablement; reps create their own materials and learn through trial and error
evidence_criteria: No sales playbooks, battlecards, or training materials exist

descriptor: Some sales materials exist but are outdated or hard to find; training is ad hoc
evidence_criteria: Basic pitch deck and product sheets exist; some onboarding material for new reps

descriptor: Organized sales enablement with current content library, onboarding program, and regular training
evidence_criteria: Central content library maintained; structured onboarding program with defined milestones; quarterly training sessions; battlecards for top competitors

descriptor: Strategic enablement with just-in-time content, deal-stage-specific resources, and skills coaching
evidence_criteria: Content mapped to buyer journey and deal stages; just-in-time content delivery through CRM; skills-based coaching program; enablement impact measured (ramp time, win rate)

descriptor: AI-powered enablement with personalized coaching, real-time call intelligence, and continuous optimization
evidence_criteria: AI-powered content recommendations; conversation intelligence tools deployed; personalized coaching plans; enablement ROI measured; new hire ramp time <3 months to full productivity

descriptor: No structured onboarding; customers figure out the product on their own
evidence_criteria: No onboarding process or welcome sequence; customer activation is undefined

descriptor: Basic onboarding exists but is inconsistent; some welcome emails or kickoff calls
evidence_criteria: Welcome email or kickoff call exists; some setup documentation available

descriptor: Structured onboarding with defined milestones, success criteria, and time-to-value tracking
evidence_criteria: Onboarding playbook with defined steps; activation milestones identified; time-to-value metric tracked; onboarding completion rate measured; customer health score initiated during onboarding

descriptor: Scalable onboarding with segmented programs, automated touchpoints, and proactive health monitoring
evidence_criteria: Segment-specific onboarding paths; automated onboarding workflows; proactive intervention when milestones missed; onboarding satisfaction survey; time-to-value consistently improving

descriptor: Self-service and guided onboarding with predictive success indicators and personalized journeys
evidence_criteria: Self-service onboarding for appropriate segments; predictive models identify at-risk onboardings; personalized onboarding journeys based on user behavior; onboarding NPS >60; <5% early churn during onboarding

descriptor: No proactive account management; customer contact only for renewals or complaints
evidence_criteria: No account management process; no tracking of account health or expansion opportunities

descriptor: Some account management activity but reactive; basic renewal tracking
evidence_criteria: Renewal dates tracked; some periodic check-ins with larger accounts

descriptor: Proactive account management with regular reviews, health scoring, and expansion opportunity identification
evidence_criteria: Quarterly business reviews with key accounts; customer health score tracked; expansion opportunities identified and pursued; NRR (Net Revenue Retention) measured; account plans for top accounts

descriptor: Strategic account management with multi-threaded relationships, success plans, and expansion playbooks
evidence_criteria: Multi-threaded relationships in key accounts; customer success plans with measurable outcomes; expansion playbooks by segment; NRR >110%; customer advocacy program feeding referrals

descriptor: Customer-as-partner model with co-innovation, executive sponsorship, and predictive retention management
evidence_criteria: Executive sponsorship program for strategic accounts; co-innovation or customer advisory board; predictive churn models; NRR >120%; account management influences product roadmap

descriptor: No channel or partnership strategy; all revenue through direct sales
evidence_criteria: No partner program or channel sales exist; 100% direct sales model

descriptor: Some informal partnerships but no structured channel strategy
evidence_criteria: A few ad hoc partnerships exist; no formal partner program or agreements

descriptor: Channel strategy defined with partner tiers, onboarding process, and revenue tracking
evidence_criteria: Partner program documented with tiers; partner onboarding process exists; partner-sourced revenue tracked; at least 3 active partners

descriptor: Mature channel program with partner enablement, co-selling, and mutual business planning
evidence_criteria: Partner enablement materials and training; co-selling motions documented; mutual business plans with top partners; partner-sourced revenue >15% of total; partner satisfaction measured

descriptor: Strategic ecosystem with technology integrations, marketplace presence, and partner-led market expansion
evidence_criteria: Technology integration partnerships with APIs; marketplace listing(s); partner-led market expansion into new segments or geos; partner-sourced revenue >30%; ecosystem strategy at board level

descriptor: No structured sales compensation; pay is ad hoc or salary-only
evidence_criteria: No commission plan or incentive structure documented

descriptor: Basic commission structure exists but not tied to strategic objectives
evidence_criteria: Commission plan documented; basic quota and commission rate defined

descriptor: Compensation plan aligned with strategic goals with clear quotas, accelerators, and SPIFs
evidence_criteria: Comp plan tied to company objectives; quota methodology documented; accelerators for overachievement; SPIFs for strategic priorities; plan communicated clearly to team

descriptor: Optimized compensation with multi-variable plans, retention bonuses, and competitive benchmarking
evidence_criteria: Multi-variable comp (new business, expansion, retention); competitive benchmarking performed; retention mechanisms included; comp plan effectiveness measured (quota attainment distribution, turnover)

descriptor: Dynamic compensation model with real-time performance tracking, predictive quota setting, and total rewards optimization
evidence_criteria: Real-time comp and performance dashboards; predictive quota models based on territory potential; total rewards philosophy documented; comp plan drives desired behaviors (verified with data); annual comp benchmarking

descriptor: No CRM or sales tools; tracking done in spreadsheets or not at all
evidence_criteria: No CRM system; pipeline tracked in spreadsheets or founder's memory

descriptor: CRM implemented but poorly adopted with inconsistent data entry
evidence_criteria: CRM system in place; <50% of deals consistently tracked; basic contact and deal data entered

descriptor: CRM well-adopted with standardized fields, pipeline views, and basic automation
evidence_criteria: CRM adoption >80%; standardized fields and picklists; pipeline dashboards; basic workflow automation (e.g., task creation, notifications); data quality standards enforced

descriptor: Integrated sales tech stack with enrichment, sequencing, analytics, and cross-system data flow
evidence_criteria: Sales tech stack mapped and documented; data enrichment integrated; outreach sequencing tools deployed; analytics across full funnel; CRM integrated with marketing automation and support

descriptor: AI-enhanced sales stack with predictive insights, automated data capture, and continuous optimization
evidence_criteria: AI-powered CRM insights (deal scoring, next-best-action); automated activity capture; real-time competitive intelligence; tech stack ROI measured; sales ops team managing continuous optimization

descriptor: No defined brand positioning; messaging is inconsistent across channels
evidence_criteria: No brand guidelines or positioning document; messaging changes with each touchpoint

descriptor: Basic brand elements exist (logo, colors) but positioning and messaging are informal
evidence_criteria: Logo and visual identity defined; some messaging exists but not consistently applied

descriptor: Brand positioning documented with clear messaging framework, voice guidelines, and channel consistency
evidence_criteria: Brand positioning document with value proposition, differentiators, and messaging pillars; brand voice guidelines; messaging applied consistently across website, sales, and marketing materials

descriptor: Strategic brand management with audience-specific messaging, brand tracking, and competitive differentiation
evidence_criteria: Audience-specific messaging variants; brand tracking metrics (awareness, perception); competitive differentiation clearly articulated; brand consistency audit performed; brand training for customer-facing teams

descriptor: Category-defining brand with thought leadership, emotional connection, and premium positioning
evidence_criteria: Brand recognized as category leader or creator; brand drives pricing power; thought leadership program with executive visibility; brand equity measured and growing; brand influences talent acquisition

descriptor: No demand generation strategy; marketing is reactive or event-driven
evidence_criteria: No demand gen plan or campaigns; marketing activity is sporadic

descriptor: Some demand generation activity but disconnected from sales and pipeline goals
evidence_criteria: Some campaigns run (email, events, content); basic lead capture in place; limited connection to pipeline targets

descriptor: Integrated demand gen strategy with multi-channel campaigns, lead nurturing, and pipeline targets
evidence_criteria: Demand gen plan with channel mix; campaigns tied to pipeline targets; lead nurturing sequences active; MQL-to-pipeline conversion tracked; marketing and sales SLA defined

descriptor: Optimized demand gen with channel ROI optimization, ABM, and full-funnel marketing
evidence_criteria: Channel-level ROI tracked and optimized; ABM program for target accounts; full-funnel demand gen (awareness through opportunity); demand gen budget allocation data-driven; pipeline contribution targets met

descriptor: Predictive demand generation with intent-based activation, dynamic budgeting, and self-optimizing campaigns
evidence_criteria: Intent data driving campaign targeting; dynamic budget allocation based on performance; self-optimizing campaign workflows; demand gen drives >50% of qualified pipeline; predictive pipeline models

descriptor: No content strategy; content created reactively or not at all
evidence_criteria: No content calendar or plan; content is ad hoc blog posts or none

descriptor: Some content produced but without strategy or audience focus
evidence_criteria: Blog posts or materials produced sporadically; no editorial calendar; content not tied to buyer journey

descriptor: Content strategy aligned with buyer journey, consistent production cadence, and SEO fundamentals
evidence_criteria: Content strategy document with audience and topic pillars; editorial calendar maintained; content mapped to buyer journey stages; basic SEO optimization; monthly content production cadence

descriptor: Data-driven content program with performance optimization, content repurposing, and thought leadership
evidence_criteria: Content performance tracked (traffic, engagement, conversion); top-performing content optimized and repurposed; thought leadership pieces from executives; content influences pipeline with attribution; multimedia formats used

descriptor: Content engine with programmatic production, AI-assisted creation, and industry-leading organic reach
evidence_criteria: Programmatic content production at scale; AI tools integrated into content workflow; organic traffic growth outpacing industry; content community built; content drives >20% of qualified leads; original research published

descriptor: No digital marketing operations; online presence is minimal
evidence_criteria: No active digital campaigns; website exists but no marketing automation or paid channels

descriptor: Basic digital marketing with some channels active but limited tracking
evidence_criteria: At least one digital channel active (email, social, SEM); basic website analytics; some email campaigns sent

descriptor: Multi-channel digital marketing with campaign tracking, A/B testing, and marketing automation
evidence_criteria: Three or more digital channels active; marketing automation platform in use; A/B testing for emails and landing pages; UTM tracking standardized; campaign performance dashboards

descriptor: Sophisticated digital operations with personalization, multi-touch attribution, and conversion optimization
evidence_criteria: Personalization across channels; multi-touch attribution model; conversion rate optimization program; retargeting and audience segmentation; digital marketing contributes measurably to pipeline

descriptor: AI-driven digital marketing with predictive audiences, dynamic creative, and omnichannel orchestration
evidence_criteria: AI-driven audience modeling and creative optimization; omnichannel campaign orchestration; real-time personalization; CAC continuously optimized; digital marketing benchmarked against industry leaders

descriptor: No marketing analytics; no understanding of what marketing activities drive results
evidence_criteria: No analytics dashboards or reporting; marketing ROI unknown

descriptor: Basic analytics with channel-level metrics but no attribution to pipeline or revenue
evidence_criteria: Google Analytics or similar tool in place; basic metrics tracked (traffic, email opens); no pipeline attribution

descriptor: Marketing analytics with pipeline attribution, campaign ROI tracking, and regular reporting
evidence_criteria: Marketing pipeline attribution model (first-touch or last-touch); campaign ROI tracked; monthly marketing analytics report; key metrics defined (MQLs, conversion rates, CAC)

descriptor: Advanced analytics with multi-touch attribution, predictive modeling, and marketing-finance alignment
evidence_criteria: Multi-touch attribution model implemented; predictive lead scoring; marketing contribution to revenue clearly quantified; marketing and finance aligned on metrics; cohort-based campaign analysis

descriptor: Full marketing measurement with unified data, real-time optimization, and incrementality testing
evidence_criteria: Unified marketing data platform; real-time campaign optimization; incrementality testing (e.g., geo-experiments, holdout groups); marketing mix modeling; marketing spend optimization automated; board-level marketing ROI reporting

descriptor: No customer advocacy programs; referrals happen organically and untracked
evidence_criteria: No referral program; no customer case studies or testimonials collected systematically

descriptor: Some testimonials and case studies collected ad hoc; informal referral asks
evidence_criteria: A few customer testimonials or case studies exist; occasional referral requests made

descriptor: Structured advocacy program with case study pipeline, referral tracking, and review generation
evidence_criteria: Customer advocacy program documented; case study production pipeline; referral program with tracking; online review generation strategy; advocacy activities tracked

descriptor: Active advocacy community with customer champions, co-marketing, and measurable referral revenue
evidence_criteria: Customer champion program with defined tiers; co-marketing initiatives with customers; referral revenue tracked; customer advisory board active; NPS promoters systematically engaged

descriptor: Customer-led growth engine with community, earned media, and advocacy-driven pipeline
evidence_criteria: Active customer community platform; earned media from customer stories; advocacy drives >15% of new pipeline; customer-led events or content; advocacy ROI measured and optimized

descriptor: No market research; market assumptions untested
evidence_criteria: No market research conducted; TAM/SAM unknown or guessed

descriptor: Basic market sizing and occasional competitive research
evidence_criteria: TAM/SAM estimated; some secondary research conducted; basic market segmentation discussed

descriptor: Regular market research with defined segments, buyer personas, and trend monitoring
evidence_criteria: Market segmentation documented with sizing; buyer personas created and validated; quarterly trend monitoring; competitive landscape updated; research informs strategy

descriptor: Continuous market intelligence with primary research, segment-level strategy, and market modeling
evidence_criteria: Primary research (surveys, interviews) conducted regularly; segment-level go-to-market strategies; market models with growth projections; market insights drive product and sales strategy; win/loss analysis by segment

descriptor: Predictive market analysis with real-time signals, emerging segment identification, and market-shaping strategies
evidence_criteria: Real-time market signal monitoring; emerging segment identification before competitors; market-shaping thought leadership; predictive market models; market research function or partnership in place; insights influence board strategy

descriptor: No marketing technology beyond basic email; manual processes
evidence_criteria: No marketing automation platform; email sent from personal accounts or basic tools

descriptor: Marketing automation platform in place but underutilized
evidence_criteria: Marketing automation tool deployed; basic email campaigns set up; limited integration with CRM

descriptor: Marketing tech stack operational with automation, CRM integration, and campaign management
evidence_criteria: Marketing automation with lead scoring and nurture workflows; CRM integration bidirectional; campaign tracking and attribution; tech stack documented; at least 3 tools in active use

descriptor: Optimized martech stack with cross-platform data flow, advanced segmentation, and usage analytics
evidence_criteria: Cross-platform data flow automated; advanced segmentation and personalization; martech stack ROI tracked; usage analytics across tools; stack rationalization performed annually

descriptor: Integrated martech ecosystem with CDP, real-time orchestration, and AI-powered optimization
evidence_criteria: Customer data platform (CDP) in place; real-time cross-channel orchestration; AI-powered optimization for campaigns; martech influences >60% of marketing activities; continuous vendor evaluation and optimization

descriptor: No PR or communications strategy; media interactions are unplanned
evidence_criteria: No PR plan or media list; no spokesperson designated

descriptor: Basic PR awareness with occasional press releases or media outreach
evidence_criteria: Some press releases issued; basic media list exists; occasional media mentions

descriptor: PR strategy with media relations plan, spokesperson training, and crisis communication basics
evidence_criteria: PR strategy documented; media relations plan with target outlets; spokesperson identified and trained; crisis communication plan exists; monthly PR activity tracked

descriptor: Proactive PR with thought leadership placement, analyst relations, and reputation monitoring
evidence_criteria: Executive thought leadership in target publications; analyst relations program; media monitoring and sentiment tracking; PR tied to business outcomes; bylined articles and speaking engagements secured

descriptor: Strategic communications with narrative control, category creation, and integrated reputation management
evidence_criteria: Category creation or narrative leadership in market; integrated comms across PR, social, and exec visibility; real-time reputation monitoring; crisis simulation exercises conducted; comms strategy reviewed at board level

descriptor: No event or community strategy; participation is reactive
evidence_criteria: No events planned; no community initiatives

descriptor: Sporadic event attendance or hosting; early community exploration
evidence_criteria: Attendance at some industry events; initial community channel (Slack, Discord) or meetup explored

descriptor: Event calendar and community program with regular engagement and ROI tracking
evidence_criteria: Annual event calendar with owned and sponsored events; community platform active with regular content; event ROI tracked (leads, pipeline); community engagement metrics monitored

descriptor: Strategic event portfolio and thriving community with content flywheel and measurable business impact
evidence_criteria: Flagship owned event or conference; community drives content and referrals; event-to-pipeline attribution; community health metrics (DAU, engagement rate); user-generated content program

descriptor: Industry-defining events and self-sustaining community ecosystem
evidence_criteria: Industry-recognized conference or event series; self-sustaining community with peer-to-peer support; community influences product roadmap; events drive brand leadership; community and event ROI exceeds benchmarks

descriptor: Hiring is reactive and unstructured; no recruiting process
evidence_criteria: No defined hiring process; roles filled through personal networks only; no job descriptions

descriptor: Basic recruiting process with job postings and interviews but inconsistent evaluation
evidence_criteria: Job descriptions written; jobs posted on at least one platform; interviews conducted but without consistent rubric

descriptor: Structured recruiting with scorecards, consistent interview process, and employer branding
evidence_criteria: Structured interview process with scorecards; ATS in use; employer branding on careers page and LinkedIn; time-to-fill tracked; offer acceptance rate measured

descriptor: Strategic talent acquisition with pipeline building, diversity sourcing, and data-driven hiring
evidence_criteria: Proactive talent pipeline for critical roles; diversity sourcing strategy; hiring quality metrics tracked (new hire performance, retention); hiring manager training program; recruiting analytics dashboard

descriptor: Predictive talent acquisition with workforce planning, employer brand excellence, and AI-assisted screening
evidence_criteria: Workforce planning integrated with business strategy; employer brand recognized in industry; AI-assisted candidate screening and matching; hire quality tracked long-term; talent community nurtured for future roles

descriptor: No structured onboarding for new hires; sink-or-swim approach
evidence_criteria: No onboarding checklist or program; new hires left to figure things out

descriptor: Basic onboarding with initial orientation but no structured ramp plan
evidence_criteria: First-day orientation exists; basic IT setup and introductions; no defined ramp milestones

descriptor: Structured onboarding with 30/60/90 day plan, buddy system, and role-specific training
evidence_criteria: 30/60/90 day onboarding plan documented; buddy or mentor assigned; role-specific training modules; onboarding satisfaction survey; time-to-productivity tracked

descriptor: Comprehensive onboarding with cultural immersion, cross-functional exposure, and ramp acceleration
evidence_criteria: Cultural immersion sessions included; cross-functional rotation or exposure; ramp metrics tracked by role type; manager onboarding training; onboarding continuously improved from feedback; new hire retention >90% at 6 months

descriptor: Personalized onboarding with adaptive learning paths, predictive ramp analytics, and pre-boarding programs
evidence_criteria: Pre-boarding program before start date; adaptive learning paths based on role and experience; predictive ramp analytics; new hire cohort community; onboarding NPS >70; ramp time benchmarked and improving

descriptor: No performance management process; feedback is rare or informal
evidence_criteria: No performance reviews or feedback cycles; no documented goals or expectations

descriptor: Annual performance reviews exist but are inconsistent and backward-looking
evidence_criteria: Annual review process exists; some goal-setting occurs; reviews perceived as administrative burden

descriptor: Regular performance cycles with goal-setting, feedback, and development conversations
evidence_criteria: Quarterly or semi-annual review cycles; goals set and tracked; 360 or upward feedback incorporated; performance documentation standardized; manager training on feedback

descriptor: Continuous performance management with real-time feedback, calibration, and career pathing
evidence_criteria: Continuous feedback culture with regular 1:1s; performance calibration across teams; career paths documented for key roles; performance data influences compensation and development; low performer management process

descriptor: High-performance culture with predictive talent analytics, personalized development, and performance-linked strategy
evidence_criteria: Predictive talent analytics (attrition risk, high-potential identification); personalized development plans; performance management directly linked to company strategy; performance culture surveys show >80% satisfaction; benchmarked against best-employer standards

descriptor: Compensation set ad hoc with no market data or internal equity analysis
evidence_criteria: No compensation philosophy; pay determined case-by-case without data

descriptor: Basic salary ranges defined; some awareness of market rates
evidence_criteria: Some salary ranges established; basic benefits package; occasional market rate checks

descriptor: Compensation framework with market-benchmarked ranges, pay bands, and equity guidelines
evidence_criteria: Compensation philosophy documented; pay bands with market-benchmarked ranges; equity compensation guidelines; annual compensation review cycle; benefits package competitive for stage

descriptor: Total rewards strategy with internal equity analysis, variable comp, and competitive benchmarking
evidence_criteria: Total rewards framework (base + equity + benefits + perks); internal equity audits performed; variable compensation tied to performance; annual market benchmarking; compensation committee or process

descriptor: Leading total rewards with pay transparency, advanced equity programs, and personalized benefits
evidence_criteria: Pay transparency policy and reporting; advanced equity programs (refreshers, performance grants); personalized benefits (flexible spending, wellness); compensation philosophy drives talent strategy; annual total rewards benchmarking vs. top employers

descriptor: No defined culture or values; culture is whatever happens organically
evidence_criteria: No written values or culture documentation; no intentional culture initiatives

descriptor: Values exist on paper but are not actively reinforced or integrated into processes
evidence_criteria: Company values written down; values referenced occasionally but not embedded in hiring or reviews

descriptor: Culture and values actively maintained with integration into hiring, onboarding, and recognition
evidence_criteria: Values integrated into interview scorecards; values-aligned recognition program; culture discussed in onboarding; annual culture survey conducted; leadership models values

descriptor: Culture as strategic asset with measurement, reinforcement, and intentional evolution
evidence_criteria: Culture metrics tracked (eNPS, engagement, values alignment); values reinforced through stories and rituals; culture considerations in M&A or major decisions; culture workshops or offsites; leaders held accountable for culture

descriptor: Industry-recognized culture with employer brand differentiation and culture-led competitive advantage
evidence_criteria: Culture recognized externally (awards, press, glassdoor); culture drives talent acquisition advantage; culture influences customer perception; culture evolution managed proactively; culture health reported to board

descriptor: No learning and development programs; growth is self-directed only
evidence_criteria: No L&D budget, programs, or resources; no mentorship or training

descriptor: Some training available but not systematic; occasional conference attendance
evidence_criteria: Ad hoc training or conference attendance; small L&D budget exists; no learning paths defined

descriptor: Structured L&D with defined learning paths, mentorship program, and dedicated budget
evidence_criteria: L&D budget allocated per employee; learning paths for key roles; mentorship program active; training completion tracked; skills gap assessment performed

descriptor: Strategic L&D tied to business needs with leadership development, skills academies, and impact measurement
evidence_criteria: L&D programs aligned to strategic skill gaps; leadership development program; internal skills academies or certification tracks; L&D impact measured (skill improvement, promotion readiness); learning culture visible

descriptor: Learning organization with personalized development, knowledge management, and continuous skill evolution
evidence_criteria: Personalized learning plans with AI recommendations; knowledge management system; internal mobility enabled by skills; L&D ROI measured; learning embedded in daily work; organization recognized for talent development

descriptor: Organizational structure evolved organically with unclear roles and reporting lines
evidence_criteria: No org chart; roles and responsibilities unclear; reporting lines ambiguous

descriptor: Basic org chart exists but doesn't reflect how work actually gets done
evidence_criteria: Org chart created; basic role descriptions exist; some role overlap or gaps acknowledged

descriptor: Intentional org design with documented roles, clear reporting lines, and span-of-control guidelines
evidence_criteria: Org design aligned to strategy; role descriptions with responsibilities and decision rights; span of control guidelines; org chart current and shared; design reviewed at least annually

descriptor: Strategic org design with cross-functional collaboration structures, capacity planning, and role leveling
evidence_criteria: Cross-functional collaboration mechanisms (squads, pods, guilds); capacity planning by function; role leveling framework; org design adapts to strategic changes; design informed by operating model

descriptor: Adaptive organizational design with network structures, dynamic teaming, and continuous reorganization capability
evidence_criteria: Network or matrix structures operating effectively; dynamic team formation for initiatives; org design agility demonstrated (reorgs executed smoothly); org effectiveness measured; design benchmarked against best practices

descriptor: No measurement of employee engagement; turnover causes unknown
evidence_criteria: No engagement survey or pulse checks; employee attrition not tracked by cause

descriptor: Informal awareness of engagement issues; exit interviews sometimes conducted
evidence_criteria: Some exit interviews conducted; informal sense of morale; attrition tracked at basic level

descriptor: Regular engagement surveys with action planning and retention metric tracking
evidence_criteria: Annual or bi-annual engagement survey; results shared with leaders; action plans created for low-scoring areas; voluntary attrition rate tracked; retention initiatives in place

descriptor: Continuous engagement monitoring with pulse surveys, predictive attrition modeling, and stay interviews
evidence_criteria: Quarterly pulse surveys; predictive attrition models for key talent; stay interviews for high performers; engagement action plans tracked to completion; manager-level engagement scores reviewed; eNPS >30

descriptor: Industry-leading engagement with real-time sentiment, personalized retention, and engagement-performance correlation
evidence_criteria: Real-time engagement sentiment monitoring; personalized retention strategies for critical talent; engagement correlated with performance and business outcomes; voluntary attrition below industry benchmark; engagement innovation (e.g., sabbaticals, radical flexibility)

descriptor: No succession planning; single points of failure exist across the organization
evidence_criteria: No succession plans for any role; key-person dependencies not identified

descriptor: Informal awareness of key-person risk; some backup responsibilities discussed
evidence_criteria: Key-person risks informally acknowledged; some cross-training for critical functions

descriptor: Succession plans for critical roles with identified successors and development activities
evidence_criteria: Critical roles identified; at least one successor identified per critical role; development plans for successors; succession review conducted annually; cross-training program for key functions

descriptor: Comprehensive succession planning with talent calibration, readiness assessment, and accelerated development
evidence_criteria: Talent calibration sessions across leadership; succession readiness assessed (ready now, ready in 1-2 years); accelerated development programs for high-potentials; succession plan covers two levels deep; emergency succession plan documented

descriptor: Strategic workforce planning with pipeline analytics, internal mobility, and leadership bench strength
evidence_criteria: Leadership bench strength metric tracked; internal promotion rate for critical roles >60%; workforce planning integrated with business strategy; succession pipeline analytics; board reviews succession for C-suite; talent marketplace active

descriptor: No DEI initiatives; workforce composition not tracked
evidence_criteria: No DEI goals, programs, or demographic tracking

descriptor: Basic awareness of DEI importance; initial data collection
evidence_criteria: Workforce demographic data collected; DEI discussed at leadership level; basic awareness training offered

descriptor: DEI strategy with goals, programs, and regular reporting on workforce composition
evidence_criteria: DEI strategy documented with measurable goals; ERGs or affinity groups supported; bias training for hiring managers; demographic reporting regular; inclusive hiring practices implemented

descriptor: Embedded DEI with inclusive culture practices, supplier diversity, and equity audits
evidence_criteria: DEI embedded in performance reviews and leadership evaluation; pay equity audits conducted; supplier diversity program; inclusive product design practices; DEI metrics reviewed by leadership quarterly

descriptor: Industry-leading DEI with systemic inclusion, external advocacy, and measurable representation progress
evidence_criteria: Representation at all levels meets or exceeds industry benchmarks; systemic inclusion practices; DEI influences business strategy; external DEI advocacy or reporting; recognized externally for DEI excellence; board diversity achieved

descriptor: No intentional architecture; system grew organically without design principles
evidence_criteria: No architecture documentation; no design reviews; system is a monolith with no clear boundaries

descriptor: Basic awareness of architecture concerns; some documentation of system components
evidence_criteria: High-level system diagram exists; some architectural decisions documented; awareness of technical constraints

descriptor: Documented architecture with design principles, ADRs, and regular architecture reviews
evidence_criteria: Architecture documentation current; Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) maintained; design principles defined; architecture review process for major changes; capacity planning based on architecture

descriptor: Strategic architecture with modularity, scalability planning, and cross-team alignment
evidence_criteria: Modular architecture supporting independent deployments; scalability roadmap; cross-team architecture alignment forums; architecture fitness functions or quality metrics; migration strategy for legacy components

descriptor: Evolutionary architecture with fitness functions, continuous modernization, and industry-leading practices
evidence_criteria: Evolutionary architecture practices with automated fitness functions; continuous modernization without major rewrites; architecture enables business agility; architecture benchmarked against industry; architecture guild or center of excellence

descriptor: No engineering standards; each developer works with their own style and practices
evidence_criteria: No coding standards, style guides, or review processes; inconsistent code quality

descriptor: Basic coding conventions discussed; code reviews happen sometimes
evidence_criteria: Some informal coding conventions; code reviews conducted for some PRs; linting partially configured

descriptor: Documented standards with mandatory code reviews, linting, and automated quality checks
evidence_criteria: Coding standards documented and enforced by linters; mandatory code reviews for all PRs; automated quality gates in CI; testing requirements defined; onboarding guide for engineering practices

descriptor: Mature engineering practices with DORA metrics tracking, engineering RFCs, and developer experience focus
evidence_criteria: DORA metrics tracked (deployment frequency, lead time, failure rate, MTTR); engineering RFC process for significant changes; developer experience surveys; pair programming or mob sessions; engineering blog or knowledge sharing

descriptor: Engineering excellence with industry-leading practices, open-source contribution, and continuous improvement culture
evidence_criteria: DORA metrics at elite level; open-source contributions; engineering brand attracts talent; continuous improvement retrospectives with data; engineering practices benchmarked against industry leaders; internal tooling and DX investment

descriptor: Technical debt not tracked or acknowledged; codebase quality degrading
evidence_criteria: No awareness of technical debt; no tracking or prioritization of debt remediation

descriptor: Some awareness of technical debt; occasional cleanup efforts
evidence_criteria: Team discusses tech debt informally; some cleanup tasks created ad hoc

descriptor: Technical debt tracked with regular allocation of time for remediation
evidence_criteria: Tech debt backlog maintained; percentage of sprint capacity allocated to debt reduction (e.g., 20%); debt items categorized by severity; debt review in sprint planning

descriptor: Strategic debt management with quantified impact, architectural remediation plans, and prevention practices
evidence_criteria: Tech debt impact quantified (developer velocity, incident rate); architectural remediation roadmap; debt prevention practices (design reviews, coding standards); tech debt metrics tracked over time; leadership visibility into debt levels

descriptor: Proactive debt prevention with automated detection, continuous refactoring, and debt-free delivery targets
evidence_criteria: Automated tech debt detection tools; continuous refactoring culture; debt-free delivery standards for new features; tech debt ratio improving quarter-over-quarter; debt management integrated into engineering OKRs

descriptor: No security practices; compliance requirements unknown or ignored
evidence_criteria: No security policies; no vulnerability scanning; compliance requirements not identified

descriptor: Basic security awareness; some compliance requirements identified
evidence_criteria: Basic security measures (HTTPS, password policies); key compliance requirements listed; no regular security audits

descriptor: Security program with vulnerability management, access controls, and compliance tracking
evidence_criteria: Vulnerability scanning regular; access control policies documented; compliance requirements tracked; security training for developers; incident response plan exists; data classification policy

descriptor: Mature security program with penetration testing, SOC 2 compliance, and security-by-design practices
evidence_criteria: Annual penetration testing; SOC 2 Type II or equivalent compliance; security champions program; secure SDLC practices; security metrics dashboard; third-party security reviews; bug bounty or responsible disclosure program

descriptor: Security-first culture with zero-trust architecture, continuous compliance, and threat modeling at design phase
evidence_criteria: Zero-trust security model; continuous compliance monitoring; threat modeling in design phase; red team exercises; security influences architecture decisions; CISO or security leader at executive level; compliance automation

descriptor: Infrastructure is fragile and manually managed; outages are frequent
evidence_criteria: No infrastructure-as-code; manual server management; frequent unplanned downtime

descriptor: Basic cloud infrastructure but limited automation and scalability planning
evidence_criteria: Cloud hosting in use; basic backup procedures; some documentation of infrastructure; scaling is manual

descriptor: Infrastructure-as-code with auto-scaling, disaster recovery plan, and uptime SLAs
evidence_criteria: IaC for core infrastructure (Terraform, CloudFormation, etc.); auto-scaling configured for key services; DR plan documented and tested annually; uptime SLAs defined; infrastructure costs tracked

descriptor: Mature infrastructure with multi-region deployment, capacity planning, and cost optimization
evidence_criteria: Multi-region or multi-AZ deployment; capacity planning with growth projections; cost optimization program (reserved instances, rightsizing); infrastructure security hardened; GitOps workflow for infrastructure changes

descriptor: Cloud-native infrastructure with serverless patterns, edge computing, and predictive scaling
evidence_criteria: Cloud-native architecture with serverless where appropriate; edge computing for performance; predictive auto-scaling; infrastructure cost per customer tracked; 99.99% uptime achieved; FinOps practice established

descriptor: No CI/CD pipeline; deployments are manual and risky
evidence_criteria: No automated build or deployment; code deployed manually; deployments are stressful events

descriptor: Basic CI pipeline with some automated tests; deployments still largely manual
evidence_criteria: CI server runs builds and some tests; deployment partially automated; deployment frequency weekly or less

descriptor: Full CI/CD pipeline with automated testing, staging environments, and defined release process
evidence_criteria: Automated build, test, and deployment pipeline; staging environment mirrors production; deployment frequency at least weekly; rollback procedure documented; feature flags for risk mitigation

descriptor: Advanced CI/CD with canary deployments, automated rollback, and deployment metrics
evidence_criteria: Canary or blue-green deployments; automated rollback on failure; deployment frequency daily or more; deployment success rate >98%; pipeline performance metrics tracked; multiple environment promotion

descriptor: Continuous deployment with zero-downtime releases, progressive delivery, and self-healing pipelines
evidence_criteria: Continuous deployment to production multiple times daily; zero-downtime deployments; progressive delivery (feature flags, A/B); self-healing pipeline with auto-remediation; deployment lead time <1 hour; DORA deployment metrics at elite level

descriptor: No monitoring or observability; issues discovered by customers
evidence_criteria: No monitoring tools; no alerting; incidents discovered through customer complaints

descriptor: Basic monitoring with uptime checks and some error tracking
evidence_criteria: Uptime monitoring active; basic error tracking (e.g., Sentry); some log aggregation; limited alerting

descriptor: Comprehensive monitoring with dashboards, structured logging, and on-call rotation
evidence_criteria: Application and infrastructure monitoring dashboards; structured logging with search capability; alerting with severity levels; on-call rotation established; key SLIs defined and tracked

descriptor: Full observability with distributed tracing, SLO tracking, and proactive anomaly detection
evidence_criteria: Distributed tracing implemented; SLOs defined and tracked with error budgets; anomaly detection alerting; runbooks for common incidents; observability data used for capacity planning; MTTD and MTTR tracked

descriptor: Predictive observability with AIOps, chaos engineering, and business-metric correlation
evidence_criteria: AIOps for intelligent alerting and root cause analysis; chaos engineering practiced regularly; business metrics correlated with technical metrics; observability drives architecture decisions; <5 min MTTD for critical issues; observability platform as competitive advantage

descriptor: No data architecture; data stored ad hoc in application databases
evidence_criteria: No data strategy; data siloed in application databases; no data governance

descriptor: Basic data storage but no strategic data management or quality processes
evidence_criteria: Database design exists; some data exports for analysis; no data quality processes; limited analytics capability

descriptor: Data architecture with warehouse, ETL pipelines, data quality checks, and access governance
evidence_criteria: Data warehouse or lakehouse in place; ETL/ELT pipelines documented; data quality checks automated; data access policies defined; self-service analytics for key users; data catalog started

descriptor: Mature data platform with real-time streaming, data mesh principles, and advanced analytics
evidence_criteria: Real-time data streaming where needed; data mesh or domain-owned data products; advanced analytics (ML models, predictive); data lineage tracked; data SLAs defined; data team or function established

descriptor: Data-driven organization with data products, AI/ML platform, and data monetization capabilities
evidence_criteria: Data products with internal and external consumers; ML/AI platform for model development and deployment; data monetization explored; data governance automated; data literacy across organization; CDO or data leader at executive level

descriptor: No API strategy; integrations are point-to-point and fragile
evidence_criteria: No documented APIs; integrations built ad hoc; no API standards

descriptor: Some APIs exist but without consistent design standards or documentation
evidence_criteria: Some REST or GraphQL APIs exist; limited documentation; no versioning strategy

descriptor: API-first approach with consistent design standards, documentation, and versioning
evidence_criteria: API design standards documented (naming, error handling, pagination); API documentation generated (OpenAPI/Swagger); versioning strategy defined; authentication and rate limiting in place; key integrations documented

descriptor: API platform with developer portal, SDK generation, and partner integration program
evidence_criteria: Developer portal or documentation site; SDKs generated for key languages; partner integration program; API analytics (usage, latency, errors); API gateway in place; API lifecycle management

descriptor: API ecosystem with marketplace, event-driven architecture, and API-as-product strategy
evidence_criteria: API marketplace or ecosystem; event-driven architecture with webhooks and pub/sub; API monetization or API-as-product strategy; API governance automated; real-time API health monitoring; API strategy at board level

descriptor: Technology vendors selected without evaluation; no vendor management
evidence_criteria: No vendor evaluation process; no contract tracking; vendor relationships unmanaged

descriptor: Some vendor evaluation for major purchases but no ongoing management
evidence_criteria: Basic evaluation for large purchases; contracts tracked in spreadsheet; vendor reviews rare

descriptor: Vendor management with evaluation criteria, contract tracking, and regular performance reviews
evidence_criteria: Vendor evaluation scorecard; contract management system or tracker; annual vendor performance reviews; SLAs defined in contracts; vendor inventory maintained

descriptor: Strategic vendor management with TCO analysis, risk assessment, and vendor consolidation planning
evidence_criteria: TCO analysis for major vendors; vendor risk assessment; vendor consolidation roadmap; strategic vs. tactical vendor tiers; vendor relationship management process; multi-vendor sourcing for critical services

descriptor: Vendor ecosystem management with strategic partnerships, continuous optimization, and vendor innovation leverage
evidence_criteria: Strategic vendor partnerships with shared roadmaps; continuous vendor optimization program; vendor innovation programs leveraged; vendor management KPIs at executive level; vendor portfolio reviewed quarterly

descriptor: No disaster recovery or business continuity plans; single points of failure everywhere
evidence_criteria: No DR plan; no backups tested; no BCP documented

descriptor: Basic backups in place; DR discussed but not documented or tested
evidence_criteria: Regular backups performed; some awareness of DR needs; no formal DR or BCP documents

descriptor: DR and BCP documented with defined RTOs/RPOs, backup testing, and communication plans
evidence_criteria: DR plan documented with RTOs and RPOs; backups tested quarterly; BCP with communication plan; critical systems identified; recovery procedures documented
