descriptor: Tested DR with automated failover, regular tabletop exercises, and cross-region redundancy
evidence_criteria: Automated failover for critical systems; DR tested bi-annually with tabletop exercises; cross-region or cross-AZ redundancy; BCP tested with stakeholders; lessons learned documented and incorporated

descriptor: Resilient-by-design systems with chaos engineering, automated recovery, and near-zero RPO/RTO
evidence_criteria: Near-zero RPO/RTO for critical systems; chaos engineering practiced regularly; automated recovery and self-healing; resilience SLAs met consistently; DR/BCP reviewed at board level; compliance with industry resilience standards

descriptor: No delivery methodology; projects run ad hoc without structure
evidence_criteria: No project management methodology; no defined phases or milestones; delivery is unpredictable

descriptor: Basic project management with some planning but inconsistent execution
evidence_criteria: Some project plans created; milestones defined for major projects; delivery methodology loosely followed

descriptor: Consistent delivery methodology with defined phases, ceremonies, and tracking
evidence_criteria: Delivery methodology documented (Agile, Scrum, Kanban, or hybrid); regular ceremonies (standups, retros, planning); project tracking tool in use; velocity or throughput measured; definition of done established

descriptor: Optimized delivery with predictable throughput, continuous improvement, and cross-team coordination
evidence_criteria: Predictable delivery throughput; continuous improvement from retrospectives with tracked action items; cross-team delivery coordination for large initiatives; delivery metrics dashboard; project post-mortems conducted

descriptor: High-performance delivery with flow optimization, value stream mapping, and data-driven continuous improvement
evidence_criteria: Value stream mapping optimized; flow metrics (cycle time, WIP, throughput) continuously improved; delivery forecasting highly accurate; delivery practices benchmarked against industry; teams self-organizing and autonomous

descriptor: No resource planning; people assigned to work reactively
evidence_criteria: No resource allocation process; people pulled between tasks without planning; overallocation common

descriptor: Basic resource allocation with some visibility into team capacity
evidence_criteria: Some awareness of team capacity; resource conflicts identified but resolved ad hoc; basic project staffing

descriptor: Resource planning with capacity tracking, skills matching, and quarterly allocation
evidence_criteria: Resource capacity tracked by team; skills inventory maintained; quarterly resource allocation planning; utilization rate monitored; resource conflicts escalated through defined process

descriptor: Strategic resource planning with demand forecasting, talent pooling, and capacity optimization
evidence_criteria: Demand forecasting for resource needs; talent pool for flexible staffing; capacity optimization across teams; resource allocation aligned to strategic priorities; bench strength planned for critical skills

descriptor: Dynamic resource optimization with real-time allocation, skill-based routing, and AI-assisted planning
evidence_criteria: Real-time resource visibility and allocation; skill-based work routing; predictive capacity models; resource optimization drives strategic agility; internal talent marketplace; resource planning integrated with financial planning

descriptor: No QA process; testing is minimal or nonexistent
evidence_criteria: No dedicated testing; bugs found in production; no test plans or automation

descriptor: Some manual testing before releases; basic test cases for critical flows
evidence_criteria: Manual testing performed for major releases; some test cases documented; no automation

descriptor: QA process with test plans, automated regression testing, and defect tracking
evidence_criteria: Test plans for features; automated regression test suite; defect tracking system; test coverage metrics; QA sign-off required for releases; testing environments maintained

descriptor: Advanced QA with shift-left testing, performance testing, and quality metrics program
evidence_criteria: Shift-left testing with developers writing tests; performance and load testing regular; quality metrics (defect escape rate, test coverage >70%); test automation in CI/CD; exploratory testing practiced

descriptor: Quality engineering with AI-assisted testing, continuous quality monitoring, and zero-defect culture
evidence_criteria: AI-assisted test generation and maintenance; continuous quality monitoring in production; quality gates fully automated; test coverage >85%; defect escape rate <1%; quality engineering team contributing to architecture decisions

descriptor: No process documentation; knowledge lives in people's heads
evidence_criteria: No SOPs or process documents; tribal knowledge dominates; processes inconsistent

descriptor: Some processes documented but documentation is outdated or incomplete
evidence_criteria: A few key processes documented; documentation scattered and not maintained; inconsistent format

descriptor: Key processes documented with standard templates, ownership, and review cadence
evidence_criteria: Core business processes documented as SOPs; documentation template standardized; process owners assigned; annual documentation review; documentation accessible in central location

descriptor: Comprehensive process library with workflow automation, compliance integration, and continuous updates
evidence_criteria: Process library covering >80% of operations; workflow automation for key processes; processes integrated with compliance requirements; documentation updated as part of change management; process effectiveness measured

descriptor: Living process system with automated documentation, process mining, and continuous optimization
evidence_criteria: Process mining tools identify optimization opportunities; documentation auto-generated from workflows; process performance benchmarked; process innovation program; process maturity assessed and improved continuously

descriptor: Operations are manual and inefficient; no automation efforts
evidence_criteria: Manual processes throughout; no automation initiatives; waste and inefficiency prevalent

descriptor: Some automation of repetitive tasks; basic efficiency awareness
evidence_criteria: A few tasks automated (scripts, macros); some efficiency improvements identified; ad hoc automation

descriptor: Systematic automation of repetitive processes with efficiency metrics and ROI tracking
evidence_criteria: Automation roadmap prioritized by ROI; key manual processes automated; efficiency metrics tracked (throughput, error rate, time saved); automation platform selected; automation documentation maintained

descriptor: Operational excellence program with workflow orchestration, process mining, and continuous improvement
evidence_criteria: Workflow orchestration platform; process mining to identify inefficiencies; continuous improvement program with measured outcomes; automation coverage >60% of eligible processes; operational efficiency KPIs at leadership level

descriptor: Intelligent automation with AI/ML-driven optimization, self-healing workflows, and industry-leading efficiency
evidence_criteria: AI/ML-driven process optimization; self-healing automated workflows; intelligent document processing; operational efficiency benchmarked against industry leaders; automation ROI exceeds targets; automation center of excellence

descriptor: No service level definitions; performance expectations unclear
evidence_criteria: No SLAs defined; no service level tracking; customer expectations unmanaged

descriptor: Informal service expectations; basic response time awareness
evidence_criteria: Informal response time expectations; some awareness of service quality; no formal SLAs

descriptor: SLAs defined for key services with monitoring, reporting, and escalation procedures
evidence_criteria: SLAs documented for internal and external services; SLA monitoring dashboards; regular SLA reporting; escalation procedures defined; SLA breaches tracked

descriptor: SLO-driven service management with error budgets, proactive monitoring, and service portfolio management
evidence_criteria: SLOs defined with error budgets; proactive SLA monitoring with early warning; service catalog maintained; service portfolio management; SLA performance influences architecture decisions; customer satisfaction correlated with SLAs

descriptor: Predictive service management with AI-driven optimization, dynamic SLAs, and customer-experience-driven targets
evidence_criteria: AI-driven SLA prediction and optimization; dynamic SLAs adjusted to customer needs; service experience targets beyond SLAs; predictive incident prevention; service management benchmarked against ITSM leaders; customer experience correlation measured

descriptor: No incident management process; fires are fought reactively
evidence_criteria: No incident response process; no severity classification; no post-incident reviews

descriptor: Ad hoc incident response; some escalation paths known informally
evidence_criteria: Informal incident response; some team members know escalation paths; basic communication during incidents

descriptor: Incident management with severity levels, on-call rotation, runbooks, and post-incident reviews
evidence_criteria: Incident severity classification; on-call rotation with defined responsibilities; runbooks for common incidents; post-incident reviews conducted; incident metrics tracked (MTTD, MTTR); communication templates

descriptor: Mature incident management with automated detection, blameless post-mortems, and trend analysis
evidence_criteria: Automated incident detection and alerting; blameless post-mortem culture with action items tracked; incident trend analysis; incident commander role defined; SLA compliance during incidents tracked; incident database maintained

descriptor: Proactive incident prevention with predictive analysis, chaos engineering, and automated remediation
evidence_criteria: Predictive incident analysis preventing issues before impact; chaos engineering regular; automated remediation for known scenarios; incident response time <5 min for critical; industry-leading reliability practices; incident management influences product design

descriptor: Changes made without process; uncontrolled changes cause incidents
evidence_criteria: No change management process; changes deployed without review; change-related incidents frequent

descriptor: Basic change awareness; some changes communicated beforehand
evidence_criteria: Major changes communicated to team; some change planning for large deployments; no formal approval process

descriptor: Change management with approval workflow, impact assessment, and rollback plans
evidence_criteria: Change request process with approval workflow; impact and risk assessment for changes; rollback plans required; change calendar maintained; change-related incidents tracked

descriptor: Optimized change management with automated validation, change velocity metrics, and risk-based governance
evidence_criteria: Automated change validation and testing; change velocity metrics tracked; risk-based change governance (standard vs. emergency); change success rate >95%; change management integrated with CI/CD; change advisory board or process

descriptor: Continuous change with automated governance, progressive delivery, and zero-change-failure culture
evidence_criteria: Fully automated change governance for standard changes; progressive delivery reducing change risk; change failure rate <2%; self-service change management for low-risk changes; change management practices benchmarked against industry

descriptor: No organized customer support; inquiries handled ad hoc by founders or developers
evidence_criteria: No support team or process; support requests handled by whoever is available; no ticket tracking

descriptor: Basic support with shared inbox and some response tracking
evidence_criteria: Shared email inbox for support; basic ticket tracking; response times inconsistent; limited knowledge base

descriptor: Structured support with ticketing system, SLAs, knowledge base, and escalation paths
evidence_criteria: Ticketing system with categorization; response and resolution SLAs; knowledge base with self-service articles; escalation matrix defined; CSAT measured; support hours defined

descriptor: Optimized support with multi-channel coverage, proactive support, and support analytics
evidence_criteria: Multi-channel support (email, chat, phone); proactive support based on product telemetry; first-contact resolution rate tracked; support analytics dashboard; support team training program; QA on support interactions

descriptor: AI-enhanced support with predictive assistance, self-service automation, and support-driven product improvement
evidence_criteria: AI-powered chatbot and ticket routing; predictive support interventions; self-service resolution >50%; support insights drive product improvements; NPS from support >60; support operations benchmarked against best-in-class

descriptor: No supply chain or vendor operations management; dependencies untracked
evidence_criteria: No vendor operations tracking; supply chain risks unknown; no SLAs with operational vendors

descriptor: Basic vendor tracking with some SLAs; supply chain risks informally understood
evidence_criteria: Key operational vendors listed; some SLAs in contracts; basic performance awareness

descriptor: Vendor operations management with performance tracking, SLA monitoring, and risk assessment
evidence_criteria: Vendor performance scorecards; SLA monitoring regular; supply chain risks documented; alternative vendors identified for critical services; vendor review cadence established

descriptor: Optimized vendor operations with total cost management, risk mitigation, and strategic partnerships
evidence_criteria: Total cost of vendor operations managed; risk mitigation plans for critical vendors; strategic vendor partnerships with joint planning; vendor diversification strategy; vendor operations dashboard

descriptor: Resilient vendor ecosystem with predictive risk management, continuous optimization, and strategic alignment
evidence_criteria: Predictive vendor risk management; continuous vendor optimization program; vendor ecosystem aligned with business strategy; vendor innovation leveraged; supply chain resilience tested regularly; vendor operations at board-level visibility

descriptor: No strategic planning process; strategy is reactive and undocumented
evidence_criteria: No strategic plan; no planning cadence; direction shifts based on opportunities or crises

descriptor: Annual strategy discussion but outcomes not documented or tracked
evidence_criteria: Annual strategy conversation occurs; some goals identified; limited follow-through and tracking

descriptor: Structured strategic planning with documented strategy, annual planning cycle, and progress tracking
evidence_criteria: Strategic plan documented with objectives and initiatives; annual planning cycle with quarterly reviews; strategic progress tracked; cross-functional input into planning; strategy communicated to all employees

descriptor: Dynamic strategic planning with scenario analysis, competitive strategy, and strategy deployment
evidence_criteria: Scenario analysis informing strategy; competitive strategy explicitly addressed; strategy deployed through OKRs or balanced scorecard; strategic assumptions tracked and validated; board input into strategic direction; mid-year strategy refresh

descriptor: Continuous strategic adaptation with real-time market sensing, portfolio strategy, and execution excellence
evidence_criteria: Real-time market signals influence strategic priorities; portfolio strategy across business units or product lines; strategic agility demonstrated (pivots executed successfully); strategy effectiveness measured with outcome metrics; strategic planning benchmarked against best practices

descriptor: Decisions made by HiPPO with no framework or documentation
evidence_criteria: No decision-making framework; decisions centralized in founder/CEO; no decision log

descriptor: Some awareness of decision-making patterns; occasional documentation of major decisions
evidence_criteria: Major decisions sometimes documented; some delegation of decisions; decision-making slow or inconsistent

descriptor: Decision-making framework with clear roles (RACI/DACI), documented decisions, and escalation paths
evidence_criteria: RACI or DACI framework adopted; decision log maintained for strategic decisions; escalation paths defined; decision-making authority levels documented; decisions communicated to stakeholders

descriptor: Data-driven decision-making with decision quality tracking, pre-mortem analysis, and distributed authority
evidence_criteria: Data-driven decision culture with evidence requirements; pre-mortem or decision quality reviews; distributed decision authority with clear guardrails; decision velocity tracked; reversible vs. irreversible decisions distinguished

descriptor: Optimized decision system with decision intelligence, rapid experimentation, and continuous calibration
evidence_criteria: Decision intelligence tools or processes; rapid experimentation for reversible decisions; decision quality calibration (reviewing past decisions); decision-making speed benchmarked; decision authority matrix continuously refined; decision culture is a competitive advantage

descriptor: No board or advisor management; relationships are informal and underutilized
evidence_criteria: No board materials preparation; no advisor agreements; board meetings (if any) are unstructured

descriptor: Basic board meetings with financial updates; advisors engaged ad hoc
evidence_criteria: Board meetings held with basic financial reports; some advisor relationships; no structured advisory program

descriptor: Structured board management with regular meetings, prepared materials, and advisory program
evidence_criteria: Quarterly board meetings with prepared materials; board deck template; advisory board or individual advisors with agreements; board input tracked; board composition reviewed

descriptor: Strategic board management with proactive engagement, committee structure, and board evaluation
evidence_criteria: Board committees (audit, compensation, governance); proactive board engagement between meetings; board skills matrix maintained; board self-evaluation conducted; strategic topics calendar for board; advisor ROI assessed

descriptor: High-performing board with strategic value creation, governance excellence, and succession planning
evidence_criteria: Board drives strategic value beyond oversight; governance practices benchmark against public-company standards; board succession planning; board diversity targets met; board-CEO relationship drives org performance; advisory network leveraged for deals and talent

descriptor: No risk management; risks identified only when they materialize
evidence_criteria: No risk register; no risk assessment process; risk management is firefighting

descriptor: Some awareness of key risks; occasional discussion at leadership level
evidence_criteria: Key risks informally discussed; some insurance coverage; no formal risk assessment

descriptor: Risk management with risk register, regular assessment, and mitigation plans
evidence_criteria: Risk register maintained with likelihood and impact ratings; quarterly risk review; mitigation plans for top risks; risk owners assigned; risk reporting to leadership

descriptor: Enterprise risk management with cross-functional assessment, scenario planning, and risk appetite definition
evidence_criteria: Enterprise-wide risk assessment; risk appetite statement defined; scenario planning for key risks; risk management integrated into strategic planning; risk dashboard at leadership level; emerging risks monitored

descriptor: Predictive risk management with real-time monitoring, stress testing, and risk-informed culture
evidence_criteria: Real-time risk monitoring; stress testing and simulation; risk-informed decision culture; risk management benchmarked against best practices; board-level risk committee; risk management influences investment decisions

descriptor: Executive team misaligned; different leaders pursuing different priorities
evidence_criteria: No shared executive agenda; conflicting priorities visible; executive meetings unproductive

descriptor: Basic executive coordination; some shared goals but limited alignment on priorities
evidence_criteria: Executive team meets regularly; some shared objectives; alignment breaks down in execution

descriptor: Aligned executive team with shared strategy, regular cadence, and cross-functional collaboration
evidence_criteria: Shared strategic priorities documented; regular executive meetings with structured agenda; cross-functional initiatives staffed; executive team charter or norms defined; conflict resolution process exists

descriptor: High-performing executive team with mutual accountability, strategic debates, and culture stewardship
evidence_criteria: Mutual accountability mechanisms (peer feedback, shared OKRs); constructive strategic debates; executive team actively stewards culture; team effectiveness assessed; executive offsites with strategic outcomes; external coach or facilitator used

descriptor: Cohesive executive leadership with one-team operating model, collective intelligence, and succession depth
evidence_criteria: One-team operating model demonstrated; collective intelligence in decision-making; succession depth for every exec role; executive team effectiveness benchmarked; team drives org performance beyond individual contributions; external recognition of leadership quality

descriptor: No regular meeting cadence; meetings are ad hoc and unstructured
evidence_criteria: No defined meeting schedule; meetings called reactively; no agendas or follow-up

descriptor: Some regular meetings but inconsistent attendance, agendas, and follow-through
evidence_criteria: Weekly team meetings occur; agendas sometimes prepared; action items rarely tracked

descriptor: Defined meeting cadence with weekly, monthly, and quarterly rhythms, agendas, and action tracking
evidence_criteria: Weekly, monthly, and quarterly meeting cadence documented; agendas prepared in advance; action items tracked to completion; meeting effectiveness feedback collected; clear meeting owners

descriptor: Optimized governance rhythms with tiered meeting structure, decision logging, and cadence compliance tracking
evidence_criteria: Tiered meeting structure (team, department, leadership, board); decision log maintained; cadence compliance >90%; meeting time budgets monitored; meeting quality surveys; strategic topics distributed across cadence

descriptor: High-performance governance with async-first principles, real-time dashboards, and meeting ROI optimization
evidence_criteria: Async-first culture reducing meeting load; real-time dashboards replace status meetings; meeting ROI tracked; governance rhythm continuously optimized; meeting culture benchmarked; governance enables speed without sacrificing quality

descriptor: All decisions centralized in founder/CEO; no delegation framework
evidence_criteria: No delegation of authority; CEO involved in all decisions; bottlenecks everywhere

descriptor: Some informal delegation but authority levels unclear
evidence_criteria: Some tasks delegated; authority boundaries unclear; frequent escalation back to CEO

descriptor: Delegation framework with defined authority levels, spending limits, and escalation triggers
evidence_criteria: Authority matrix documented; spending authority limits by level; escalation triggers defined; delegation communicated to team; CEO tracks delegation effectiveness

descriptor: Mature delegation with decision rights, autonomous teams, and delegation health measurement
evidence_criteria: Decision rights framework (RACI/DACI) deployed org-wide; autonomous team charters; delegation health metrics (% decisions below CEO, decision latency); delegation coaching for managers; quarterly delegation review

descriptor: Distributed authority with self-organizing teams, real-time delegation analytics, and scalable governance
evidence_criteria: Self-organizing teams with clear guardrails; real-time delegation analytics; >80% decisions made below CEO level; delegation scales with org growth; authority matrix continuously refined; delegation culture is competitive advantage

descriptor: No goal-setting framework; priorities are implicit or shifting
evidence_criteria: No OKRs, KPIs, or formal goal framework; goals not documented

descriptor: Goals set annually but not cascaded or regularly tracked
evidence_criteria: Annual goals exist; limited cascade to teams; goals reviewed infrequently; no standardized framework

descriptor: OKR or equivalent framework with quarterly goal-setting, cascading, and progress tracking
evidence_criteria: OKR or goal framework adopted; quarterly goal-setting cycle; goals cascaded from company to team level; progress tracked at least monthly; goals visible to all employees

descriptor: Mature goal system with cross-functional alignment, outcome-based metrics, and retrospective learning
evidence_criteria: Cross-functional OKR alignment; outcomes-based (not output-based) key results; quarterly retrospective on goal quality; goal completion rate tracked; goals influence resource allocation; stretch goals balanced with commitments

descriptor: Strategic goal system with real-time progress, predictive achievement, and goal-strategy integration
evidence_criteria: Real-time goal progress dashboards; predictive goal achievement models; goals directly linked to strategic plan; goal framework evolves based on retrospective data; goal-setting quality benchmarked; goal system drives organizational alignment at scale

descriptor: Legal and regulatory requirements unknown or ignored; no compliance program
evidence_criteria: No legal counsel retained; compliance requirements not identified; no policy documentation

descriptor: Basic legal awareness; outside counsel used reactively for issues
evidence_criteria: Outside counsel engaged for contracts; some awareness of regulatory requirements; basic employment law compliance; no proactive compliance program

descriptor: Compliance program with identified requirements, policy documentation, and regular legal review
evidence_criteria: Regulatory requirements identified and tracked; key policies documented (privacy, employment, IP); contract review process; annual compliance review; data privacy basics (privacy policy, consent)

descriptor: Proactive compliance with regulatory monitoring, training programs, and audit readiness
evidence_criteria: Regulatory change monitoring; compliance training for relevant employees; GDPR/CCPA or equivalent privacy compliance; audit-ready documentation; compliance risk assessment; internal or external legal counsel on retainer

descriptor: Compliance excellence with automated monitoring, regulatory relationships, and compliance-as-enabler mindset
evidence_criteria: Automated compliance monitoring; regulatory relationships proactively managed; compliance enables market entry (not just risk mitigation); compliance program benchmarked; board-level compliance reporting; GRC platform in use

descriptor: No structured stakeholder communication; information shared informally or not at all
evidence_criteria: No regular communication to employees, investors, or partners; information silos

descriptor: Periodic all-hands or updates but inconsistent and one-directional
evidence_criteria: Occasional all-hands meetings; some investor updates; communication is top-down only

descriptor: Regular stakeholder communication with defined channels, cadence, and two-way feedback
evidence_criteria: Monthly all-hands or team updates; quarterly investor updates; internal communication channels defined; two-way feedback mechanisms; communication calendar maintained; key decisions communicated promptly

descriptor: Strategic communication with tailored messaging, transparency metrics, and multi-stakeholder engagement
evidence_criteria: Stakeholder-specific communication plans; transparency metrics tracked (employee awareness, investor satisfaction); multi-directional communication; communication effectiveness surveyed; crisis communication plan; internal comms supports culture

descriptor: Radical transparency with real-time information sharing, stakeholder trust measurement, and communication excellence
evidence_criteria: Radical transparency culture with open metrics and decision rationale; real-time information dashboards for stakeholders; stakeholder trust index measured; communication drives competitive advantage in talent and investor relations; communication practices benchmarked; board recognizes communication quality
